title: Tunisian Fish Tagine
description: A savory and fluffy pescatarian-friendly dish featuring fennel seeds, this Tunisian fish tagine is perfect for meal prep. It is made with a mixture of fish, vegetables, and spices, all cooked together in a flavorful tagine sauce.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the fennel seeds, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the mortar and pestle and pound until the seeds are fragrant and well combined with the spices.
Add the pounded spice mixture to the tagine or Dutch oven and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cod fillets to the tagine or Dutch oven and spoon some of the spice mixture over the top of each fillet.
Add the cherry tomatoes to the tagine or Dutch oven and spoon some of the spice mixture over the top of the tomatoes.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven and cook over medium heat for 15 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Serve the fish and vegetables hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
